No Poverty.reason: Financial management can indirectly support access to financial services (Target 1.4), which aids poverty reduction. However, the course description doesn't explicitly focus on poverty, justifying a low score.

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to health or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: As a university-level elective course, it directly contributes to providing quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and developing relevant skills for employment (Target 4.4).

Gender Equality.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Financial Management is central to economic growth, productivity, job creation (Target 8.3), and strengthening domestic financial institutions (Target 8.10).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Financial management is essential for funding and developing quality infrastructure (Target 9.1) and increasing access to financial services for industrial enterprises (Target 9.3).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Financial management can indirectly relate to improving the regulation and monitoring of global financial markets and institutions (Target 10.5), which can help reduce inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to sustainable cities or communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to responsible consumption or production.

Climate Action.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to marine life.

Life on Land.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description links to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Financial management principles can promote transparency and accountability, indirectly contributing to reducing illicit financial flows and corruption (Targets 16.4, 16.5) and strengthening institutions.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Financial management is foundational for mobilizing financial resources (Target 17.3) and enhancing global macroeconomic stability (Target 17.13), crucial for achieving all SDGs.
